channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Blackhawks organist works his last game after 33 seasons with the team
item.description: Chicago Blackhawks organist Frank Pellico worked his last game with the team on Sunday night after spending 33 seasons with the organization.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biyendolo and Cooper score as United States beats Australia 2-1 in SheBelieves Cup
item.description: Lynn Biyendolo scored 42 seconds into the match and the United States beat Australia 2-1 on Sunday in the SheBelieves Cup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No. 1 Notre Dame's backcourt is a tough matchup. NC State's Zoe Brooks was ready for it
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Zoe Brooks never panicked, flinched or hesitated. Not with the chance to take on the All-American guards leading the nation's top-ranked team. “I like challenges,” the N.C State sophomore said. Looked that way, too.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hat trick puts Alex Ovechkin 13 away from breaking Wayne Gretzky's NHL career goals record
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Alex Ovechkin fired a perfect wrist shot past Calvin Pickard for his first goal of the game, scored from his office on the power play for his second and sailed a shot into an empty net from his own zone to complete the hat trick.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Warriors’ Kuminga ruled out for next 2 games but takes ‘huge step’ in ankle recovery
item.description: Kuminga missed his 23rd straight game with a right ankle sprain but took a 'huge step' over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Evan Webeck, The Mercury News

item.title: Travis Kelce 'probably' knows retirement decision already, his brother says
item.description: Jason Kelce dished on Travis Kelce retirement talk as the big question had swirled around the Kansas City Chiefs star during the 2024 season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin gets historic 100th World Cup race win and ties record for most podiums
item.description: SESTRIERE, Italy (AP) — Mikaela Shiffrin is 100% the best in skiing’s World Cup history book. Shiffrin’s record-extending 100th career World Cup race win Sunday fulfilled a quest put on hold by a serious crash in November.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Keyonte George scores 30 in Jazz win over Rockets
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Keyonte George scored 30 points and Lauri Markkanen added 23 points and 10 rebounds to rally the Utah Jazz to a 124-115 victory over the Houston Rockets on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Lakers snap Nuggets' 9-game winning streak behind Luka Doncic's best game yet for LA
item.description: Luka Doncic scored 32 points in his best game by far with the Lakers, leading Los Angeles to a 123-100 runaway win at Denver on Saturday night that snapped the Nuggets' nine-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kent Johnson scores 2 goals, the Blue Jackets beat the Blackhawks 5-1 to end a 4-game losing streak
item.description: Zach Werenski scored and had two assists and Kent Johnson scored twice for the Columbus Blue Jackets in a 5-1 victory against the Chicago Blackhawks on Saturday to end a four-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Booker, Durant and Beal help the Suns beat the Bulls to snap a 4-game losing streak
item.description: Devin Booker had 29 points and eight assists, Kevin Durant scored 27 points and Bradley Beal had 25 in his return to the starting lineup to help the Phoenix Suns beat the Chicago Bulls 121-117 on Saturday to end a four-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Legendary NBA coach Gregg Popovich not expected to return this season, future uncertain after stroke: report
item.description: Spurs head coach Gregg Popovich is reportedly not expected to return this season, and his future is uncertain he continues to recover from a stroke.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Juan Soto hits a 426-foot home run in his first at-bat for the New York Mets
item.description: PORT ST. LUCIE, Fla. (AP) — Juan Soto wasted little time in showing off his power for his new team. Again. Soto homered in his first spring training at-bat for the New York Mets, hitting a solo shot to left-center field in the first inning against Houston on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thunder cruise past Jazz in first game after break
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 21 points and the West-leading Oklahoma City Thunder beat the Utah Jazz 130-107 on Friday night for their eighth victory in nine games. Chet Holmgren added 20 points for the Thunder.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Towns, Brunson help Knicks edge Bulls 113-111 in OT
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Karl-Anthony Towns had 32 points and 18 rebounds, Jalen Brunson added 22 points and 12 assists and the New York Knicks edged the Chicago Bulls 113-111 in overtime Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ticket demand rapidly rising for Luka Doncic's revenge game against the Dallas Mavericks
item.description: The moment that fans of the NBA have been waiting for is just days away, and the prices to get in the arena are meeting the hype. New Los Angeles Lakers star Luka Doncic, who was acquired in a shocking trade on Feb. 1, will have his chance soon to take on his old squad.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: How Central Girls Wrestling reached the pinnacle of Colorado Wrestling
item.description:
Central Girls Wrestling, aka D51 Phoenix, featuring girls from all four D51 high schools, has done it.
They are the cream of the crop.
The top of the heap.
The best in the state.
They are the undisputed 5A State Champions.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Mastroeni prepares Real Salt Lake for a physical battle in Costa Rica
item.description: Real Salt Lake head coach Pablo Mastroeni anticipates a fierce contest as his team travels to Costa Rica for their first competitive match of 2025 — a CONCACAF Champions Cup Round One clash against reigning Costa Rican champions CS Herediano.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Cubs announce Shota Imanaga as Opening Day starter in Tokyo
item.description: The Cubs have named their 2025 Opening Day starter, and it's about the least surprising announcement possible.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: 1st-year White Sox manager Will Venable goes 1-on-1 with GN Sports from spring training
item.description: PHOENIX — As first-year White Sox manager Will Venable begins to introduce himself to Chicago baseball fans, you can probably already state this about him with certainty: He'd beat any other MLB manager on the basketball court.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500 for Hendrick Motorsports
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla (AP) — William Byron fortuitously missed the ferocious wrecks down the stretch at the Daytona 500 that knocked out contenders racing for the checkered flag and left him with a repeat victory in sight.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500
item.description: Ninth in the No. 24 Chevrolet with one lap left, Byron was in the right place by racing near the outside wall in overtime to became the first back-to-back winner since Denny Hamlin in 2019-20.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
